title: Breathing Techniques for Beginners: Simple Guide to Stress Relief & Better Sleep

longTail: how to do box breathing correctly

competitorInsights: Top results (Healthline, American Lung Association, Cleveland Clinic) provide comprehensive technique lists (7-10 methods) but share common gaps: lack scientific citations, no beginner progression frameworks, missing contraindications, and unclear technique selection guidance. Most adopt medical/clinical angles targeting people with existing conditions rather than everyday stress management.
suggestedAngle: Create a beginner-friendly starter guide focusing on 3-4 core techniques (box breathing, 4-7-8, diaphragmatic) with clear decision framework for which technique to use when (stress vs. sleep vs. focus). Include 5-minute practice routines, science-backed benefits (with citations), and practical tips for building a daily habit. Differentiate through Breathe app's evidence-based, non-esoteric approach and focus on busy professionals needing quick, effective solutions.
reasoning: Strong strategic fit for Breathe's positioning: breathing techniques are the app's core differentiator vs. meditation-only competitors. High search volume ('breathing exercises' ranks in top wellness search trends), aligns with target audience (stressed professionals seeking quick solutions), and fills content gap in existing portfolio (only workplace-specific breathing covered). Evidence-based angle and beginner focus match brand voice perfectly. Expected to drive organic traffic from informational queries and convert readers to app users seeking guided practice.
